Type: Subject Identifier for the Study  Char
Mapping Rule: Extract the last 8 characters of the Synthea ID string.
Concatenate STUDYID, a hyphen "-", and SUBJID.
Keep as YYYY-MM-DD (Ensure it is an ISO 8601 character string).

column7: Map directly. If missing, leave blank.
If DEATHDATE is not missing, set to "Y".
Uppercase the value. Map "BLACK" to "BLACK OR AFRICAN AMERICAN", "NATIVE" to "AMERICAN INDIAN OR ALASKA NATIVE". Keep "WHITE" and "ASIAN" as is.
If upcase is "NONHISPANIC" set to "NOT HISPANIC OR LATINO". If "HISPANIC" set to "HISPANIC OR LATINO".

Mapping Rule: Since all patients are in Massachusetts, hardcode as "USA".

column7: Concatenate STUDYID, "-", and the last 8 chars of PATIENT string.
Sort by USUBJID, AESTDTC, AETERM. Generate sequential number per patient (1, 2, 3...).
Map directly. If missing, leave blank.
If STOP is not missing, set to "RECOVERED/RESOLVED". If missing, set to "NOT RECOVERED/NOT RESOLVED".
